title: Algerian Apricot Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful pescatarian dish featuring apricots, perfect for a comforting meal. This tagine is a traditional Algerian recipe with a twist, incorporating fermented ingredients for added depth.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper and stir to combine.
Add the dried apricots and cook for 1-2 minutes, until they start to rehydrate.
Add the fish stock and fermented fish sauce, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20-25 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
